info.title: FortiManager JSON API Documentation
info.description:
Documentation for FortiManager JSON API, ADOM version 7.2

This module is responsible for the configuration of managed devices. FortiManager has multiple configuration sets:
  • Global (/pm/config/global/) scope is all ADOMs, and configurations are copied to ADOM during global package assignment.
  • ADOM (/pm/config/adom/) configurations are managed by packages and templates. Each template and package have their scope restricted to the a subset of the ADOM's devices that are assigned to the package, and configurations are copied down to device during install.
  • Device (/pm/config/device/global/, /pm/config/device/vdom/) scope is one device only.

Whenever configuration for Global or ADOM is modified, it will eventually be copied to Device config database after next installation. A few selected device-level objects are listed in this documentation. For other objects that are not supported by higher-level configuration sets, JSON requests to directly access and modify the configuration at the Device level can be done by a linear translation of FGT CLI object paths. Depending on OS version and platform type, the availability of objects and attributes will be different on different devices.

An example at the end of this section in documentation shows how FGT CLI translation works.

common.filter.object.description: Filter the result according to a set of criteria.

common.range.object.description: Limit the number of output. For a range of [a, n], the output will contain n elements, starting from the ath matching result.

common.sort.object.description: Specify the sorting of the returned result.

params.cdb.get.obj.option.opts.description:
Set fetch option for the request. If no option is specified, by default the attributes of the object will be returned.
scope member - Return a list of scope members along with other attributes.
chksum - Return the check-sum value instead of attributes.
datasrc - Return all objects that can be referenced by an attribute. Require attr parameter.

params.cdb.get.table.option.opts.description:
Set fetch option for the request. If no option is specified, by default the attributes of the objects will be returned.
count - Return the number of matching entries instead of the actual entry data.
scope member - Return a list of scope members along with other attributes.
datasrc - Return all objects that can be referenced by an attribute. Require attr parameter.
get reserved - Also return reserved objects in the result.
syntax - Return the attribute syntax of a table or an object, instead of the actual entry data. All filter parameters will be ignored.

auth-session-check-source-ip.description: Enable/disable checking of source IP for authentication session.

auth-timeout.description: SSL-VPN authentication timeout (1 - 259200 sec (3 days), 0 for no timeout).

auto-tunnel-static-route.description: Enable/disable to auto-create static routes for the SSL-VPN tunnel IP addresses.

banned-cipher.description: Select one or more cipher technologies that cannot be used in SSL-VPN negotiations. Only applies to TLS 1.2 and below.

browser-language-detection.description: Enable/disable overriding the configured system language based on the preferred language of the browser.

check-referer.description: Enable/disable verification of referer field in HTTP request header.

ciphersuite.description: Select one or more TLS 1.3 ciphersuites to enable. Does not affect ciphers in TLS 1.2 and below. At least one must be enabled. To disable all, set ssl-max-proto-ver to tls1-2 or below.

client-sigalgs.description: Set signature algorithms related to client authentication. Affects TLS version <= 1.2 only.

deflate-min-data-size.description: Minimum amount of data that triggers compression (200 - 65535 bytes).

dns-suffix.description: DNS suffix used for SSL-VPN clients.

dtls-hello-timeout.description: SSLVPN maximum DTLS hello timeout (10 - 60 sec, default = 10).

dtls-tunnel.description: Enable/disable DTLS to prevent eavesdropping, tampering, or message forgery.

dual-stack-mode.description: Tunnel mode: enable parallel IPv4 and IPv6 tunnel. Web mode: support IPv4 and IPv6 bookmarks in the portal.

encode-2f-sequence.description: Encode \2F sequence to forward slash in URLs.

encrypt-and-store-password.description: Encrypt and store user passwords for SSL-VPN web sessions.

force-two-factor-auth.description: Enable/disable only PKI users with two-factor authentication for SSL-VPNs.

header-x-forwarded-for.description: Forward the same, add, or remove HTTP header.

http-compression.description: Enable/disable to allow HTTP compression over SSL-VPN tunnels.

http-only-cookie.description: Enable/disable SSL-VPN support for HttpOnly cookies.

http-request-body-timeout.description: SSL-VPN session is disconnected if an HTTP request body is not received within this time (1 - 60 sec, default = 20).

http-request-header-timeout.description: SSL-VPN session is disconnected if an HTTP request header is not received within this time (1 - 60 sec, default = 20).

https-redirect.description: Enable/disable redirect of port 80 to SSL-VPN port.

idle-timeout.description: SSL-VPN disconnects if idle for specified time in seconds.

login-attempt-limit.description: SSL-VPN maximum login attempt times before block (0 - 10, default = 2, 0 = no limit).

login-block-time.description: Time for which a user is blocked from logging in after too many failed login attempts (0 - 86400 sec, default = 60).

login-timeout.description: SSLVPN maximum login timeout (10 - 180 sec, default = 30).

port-precedence.description: Enable/disable, Enable means that if SSL-VPN connections are allowed on an interface admin GUI connections are blocked on that interface.

reqclientcert.description: Enable/disable to require client certificates for all SSL-VPN users.

saml-redirect-port.description: SAML local redirect port in the machine running FortiClient (0 - 65535). 0 is to disable redirection on FGT side.

servercert.description: Name of the server certificate to be used for SSL-VPNs.

items.description: IPv6 source address of incoming traffic.

source-address6.description: IPv6 source address of incoming traffic.

source-address6-negate.description: Enable/disable negated source IPv6 address match.

items.description: SSL-VPN source interface of incoming traffic.

source-interface.description: SSL-VPN source interface of incoming traffic.

ssl-client-renegotiation.description: Enable/disable to allow client renegotiation by the server if the tunnel goes down.

transform-backward-slashes.description: Transform backward slashes to forward slashes in URLs.

tunnel-addr-assigned-method.description: Method used for assigning address for tunnel.

tunnel-connect-without-reauth.description: Enable/disable tunnel connection without re-authorization if previous connection dropped.

items.description: Names of the IPv4 IP Pool firewall objects that define the IP addresses reserved for remote clients.

tunnel-ip-pools.description: Names of the IPv4 IP Pool firewall objects that define the IP addresses reserved for remote clients.

items.description: Names of the IPv6 IP Pool firewall objects that define the IP addresses reserved for remote clients.

tunnel-ipv6-pools.description: Names of the IPv6 IP Pool firewall objects that define the IP addresses reserved for remote clients.

tunnel-user-session-timeout.description: Time out value to clean up user session after tunnel connection is dropped (1 - 255 sec, default=30).

url-obscuration.description: Enable/disable to obscure the host name of the URL of the web browser display.

ztna-trusted-client.description: Enable/disable verification of device certificate for SSLVPN ZTNA session.

items.description: IPv6 source address of incoming traffic.

source-address6.description: IPv6 source address of incoming traffic.

source-address6-negate.description: Enable/disable negated source IPv6 address match.

items.description: SSL-VPN source interface of incoming traffic.

source-interface.description: SSL-VPN source interface of incoming traffic.

attr.description: The name of the attribute to retrieve its datasource. Only used with datasrc option.

fields.description: Limit the output by returning only the attributes specified in the string array. If none specified, all attributes will be returned.

loadsub.description: Enable or disable the return of any sub-objects. If not specified, the default is to return all sub-objects.
